question.text: Under Section 15Z of the SEBI Act, what is the time limit for filing an appeal to the Supreme Court against a SAT order?

answer.text: Within 30 days from the date of communication of the SAT decision
Within 45 days from the date of communication of the SAT decision
Within 90 days from the date of communication of the SAT decision
Within 60 days from the date of communication of the SAT decision

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Section 15Z states that any person aggrieved by a SAT decision may file an appeal to the Supreme Court within 60 days from the date of communication of the decision on any question of law. **The Trap:** 45 days is the timeline for appeal to SAT, not to the Supreme Court. These two timelines are often confused. **Cross-Exam Context:** The SAT-Supreme Court appeal hierarchy is tested in NISM Series III-A and III-B. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Appeals to the Supreme Court can only be on questions of law, not on questions of fact - ensure your grounds of appeal are legally sound.
